Autumn Martin Humbaugh

n rop dSt soe l1 : g 4 u 43 51 PM3 g9 a6 l1b frt tm  g 9u eh t1 09 a1 30 3l ri  y 02 Fg   ·
This is for parents that will have a child entering 6th grade at some point in the future. I currently teach 6th grade math and I cannot stress enough that as your child prepares to enter 6th grade, one of the most important ways you can support them is by helping them master their multiplication and division facts. We use these two skills in almost everything we do… decimals, fractions, ratios, percentages, equations, area, volume, etc.
When students understand the concept of multiplying and dividing, it allows them to focus on learning new concepts instead of getting stuck on basic calculations. Strong foundations helps them build confidence, reduces frustration, and makes the 6th grade math transition so much smoother.
I would say by the time they’re in 3rd grade and begin learning these two standards, go ahead and start practicing them at that point because it will set them up for success later!
